_label_note: EU Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered public support-hotline bait, obfuscated VAT/tax/passport phone-shaped fragments, dotted-date and filing-reference phone bait, role/placeholder emails, template passport values, EU registry/ISIN/LEI/tax numeric fragments, log-id amount fragments, placeholder country-code phones, and negated MAE/contingent-MNPI language. Full EU member-state national-ID breadth and broader semantic GDPR special-category coverage remain ideal-tier gaps.
_label_tier_note: EU Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. Pending labels still require project-owner approval before promotion-ready status.

Re: EU MAR/GDPR alignment for crypto, ESG/climate, cyber, insider-list, and information-barrier workflows (EU issuer context)
This note is primarily educational and operational; examples below reuse regulatory terms without creating market sensitivity.
MAR/insider-list: Refresher on Article 18 obligations, role-based access, and template entries using titles only (no names, no contact details).
Crypto operations: Marketing will reference the “Orion NovaPay” wallet for energy-rebate disbursements; product copy avoids investment claims and contains no issuer-forward guidance.
CSRD/SFDR: Illustrative climate KPI mapping and taxonomy alignment exercises are scheduled; examples use public methodologies and do not refer to unpublished performance or targets.
Positive — non-public cyber adjacency: The issuer is assessing a suspected cyber incident impacting the validator cluster supporting our tokenised renewable certificate pilot; materiality is under review and MAR Article 17 disclosure timing is being evaluated, with a temporary insider-list maintained for the pilot team by role only.
Cyber tabletop: Separate from the above, a scheduled incident-response drill will simulate a ransomware event; training data are synthetic and will not involve live systems or client records.
Information-barrier: Cross-functional ESG taxonomy workshop will operate under a clean-team protocol; no transaction workstream or issuer strategy will be discussed.
Commercial terms: Procurement will circulate a demonstration template for supplier climate-data services; it shows contractual structures without real pricing, volumes, or counterparties.
GDPR Art 9 and transfers: HR wellness program may process special-category health data via a processor in the United States; SCCs, TIAs, and EU representative arrangements are prepared, unrelated to capital markets.
Identifiers (illustrative only): Form fields may show “ISIN: XX000000-TRAIN”, “LEI: 00000000000000000000”, and “Ticker: ORENZ”; listing venue example “Euronext Amsterdam” refers to a previously announced 2024 admission, not a new event.
Blackout and tipping: The attached training calendar uses hypothetical blackout periods and warning banners; it does not create closed periods, and no tipping risks arise from the examples.
Crypto/ESG marketing: Public blog drafts describe educational content about carbon-chain audits and smart-meter tokens; there are no undisclosed partnerships, KPIs, or commercial commitments.
Contacts: Please route queries to compliance@orion-renewables.eu or consult ESMA/AFM public helplines for general guidance.
